title: Level 684
wikitext:
{{Rewrite|date=May 20, 2026|The entity just exists and holds not much importance in the level itself right now, there also may be occasional bloat but idk - Catorze de Julho }} {{DISPLAYTITLE:Level 684: "Gallery of Floorspace"}}{{Blurbg|file=Empty_Benches_ThePMA(734_(14918368723).jpg}}{{Headerbox|file=Empty_Benches_ThePMA(73)_(14918368723).jpg|header=The galleries are nice... until you start starving.}}{{Class 3|class=3|safety=Unsafe|security=Unstable|entity=Low Entity Count|hexagon=3}} == Description == {{Blurimage|file=Empty_Benches_ThePMA(73)_(14918368723).jpg|caption=An image of one of the smaller rooms of '''Level 684'''.}}'''Level 684''', commonly referred to as the '''Gallery of Floorspace''', resembles a near-infinite museum, with rooms stretching from approximately 9.25 metres up to staggering 684 metres in height. The galleries are usually vast and empty, in rare instances, including more tightly-knit artifacts. '''Level 684''''s rooms vary in architecture and exhibitions - some rooms have modern art deco, while others are Victorian-styled. Rooms may also be themed around a certain topic or subject, such as the '''Ad Astra''' room that was discovered sometime in April 2002. The '''Ad Astra''' room is also special as it predicted the denouncing of Pluto as a planet, the Artemis missions, and will predict a new ninth and tenth planet in the solar system. On the contrary, the '''Globe Room''' (Discovered in December 2003) incorrectly lists the Soviet Union as still existing and has many other outdated remarks about world relations. Any attempt to destroy the exhibition is impossible, as the level will rapidly repair itself after being damaged. Some of the rooms in the gallery can disobey physics and geometry, with objects not being on pillars, and some concrete blocks hovering on their own. In some rooms themed around abstract art and sculptures, the sculptures occasionally swap places with each other and change positions, though they are not known to be alive and seem to just be a property of the level instead. '''Level 684''' has an extremely low amount of resources, with approximately 1/8th of the average supply count of an average backrooms level. This makes long-term habitation in it impossible. '''Level 684''' has been known for decades, and many explorations have taken place there, often with wanderers bringing bags of food along for the journey. Though rarely, those bags would inexplicably disappear, which could leave the group to starve to death. There have been 2 [[M.E.G.]] exploration group cases of this happening, with an unknown number of other survivors going through this fate, as bodies vanish soon after death, even though there are no bugs or common Backrooms entities in this level to eat the bodies. == Entities == '''Level 684''' has been thought for years to be devoid of other life, until the discovery of a certain type of entity within the corridors. === Shama' hay Men === {{Blurimage|file=Folklore_Wax_Museum_inside_Kirkuk_Qishla,_City_of_Kirkuk,_northern_Iraq_37.jpg|caption=A rare type of corridor containing the dangerous yet elusive "Shama' hay Men"}}The '''Shama' hay Men''' are a kind of entity with the appearance of a wax model that represents a culture or place. They are only very rarely found in corridors of '''Level 684''' and are usually hostile. Due to the properties of the level, they cannot be destroyed, so it is best to stay clear of the corridor they are in after spotting spot one. Despite the name, the '''Shama' hay Men''' can also be women or children or can take the shape of something non-human entirely (a pet). They may have items in their hands, such as big baskets of fruit. Even though tempting for such a food-ridden place as this, if one attempts to steal one of the entities' baskets, they will most likely be killed. Only 2 photos of the '''Shama' hay Men''' have been published due to their scarcity and dangerous nature. == Colonies and Outposts == Due to the properties of '''Level 684''' (such as its lack of object permanence and resources), no known colonies of this level have been founded, despite the high documentation it has. == Entrances and Exits == {{Blurimage|file=Staircase_inside_Nairobi_National_Museum_02.jpg|caption=A rare kind of exit that takes the form of a staircase.|width=200px}} === Entrances === *[[No-Clipping|Noclipping]] into an empty bookshelf in [[Level 353]] will lead here. *There is a rare chance of entering this level if by going through a window in [[Level 188]]. *Walking into a suddenly spacious room in [[Level 810]] and sitting on the bench in the its center will take here. *Sleeping in a room with an unnerving sculpture in [[Level 5.1]] will lead here. === Exits === *Finding a large, mostly empty gallery and falling into the ground will lead to [[Level 20]]. *Going up a rare stairwell will take one to [[Level 54]]. *Finding a room of house supplies and going through the trapdoor in one of them will lead to [[Level 193]]. *Finding a double swing doors will lead one to [[Level 366]]. *Holding a fire extinguisher in this level will take one to [[Level 1.5]]. *Finding a random Christmas tree sculpture in a dimly lit room will take one to [[Level 449]]. ---- {{Collapse|initial='''> Open Author & Licensing Information'''|final='''> Close Author & Licensing Information'''|body= '''Author''': [[User:EnphosianUnphosian|EnphosianUnphosian]]
